_meta.source: Pirate Diffusion / Graydient.ai Telegram community chat (5,194 filtered messages, May 2023 – Feb 2025)
_meta.notes: Trusted sources: Captain (API author), tlack (core dev), Hampsteri (power user / workflow creator), SquibDuck (power user), Eggs Benadryl (power user). All parameter observations are empirical unless marked [official].

images.notes: Captain confirmed /images:3 is the safe default when stacking LoRAs. Combinations of LoRAs can cause failures at higher counts. The system itself may auto-reduce. For Flux Schnell and many ComfyUI workflows the standard maximum is 9.
images.source: Captain: 'it seems doing /images:3 is the safe default'

guidance.notes: Flux models behave very differently from SD models — guidance above 3.5 on Flux often degrades quality. For Flux Schnell the guidance is fixed at 1 and ignored. Captain suggested /guidance:11 for photo realism on older models. Eggs Benadryl: 'Guidance is likely far too high' (on /guidance:15 for a Flux workflow).
guidance.override_priority: Placing #recipes before /guidance:7 lets you override recipe defaults

steps.notes: Flux Schnell steps are fixed to 4; the model ignores higher values. LTX video at low frame counts benefits from /steps:120. LCM+DMD workflows use 4–8 steps by design.

size.notes: Images above 3000px on any side cause delivery issues (Telegram API timeouts, S3 size limits). For video: /run:video (LTX+Flux) often fails above 768x768; video-medium (pure LTX) is more reliable. Hampsteri: 'The model says the limit is 1280x720, but the workflow often fails before reaching that.' For video-nsfw-q8 only 512x512 is reliable. /facelift /photo /size:1700x1700 is reliable; 1800x1800 is not always.

strength.notes: strength is only valid for /remix, /more, and img2img workflows. Do NOT include /strength in pure /render (txt2img) calls — it will leave the image 50% unfinished.

seed.batch_behavior_comfyui: All images in a ComfyUI workflow batch share one seed. Only /render (Diffusers) gives each image its own seed (incrementing by 1).
seed.notes: Hampsteri: 'Workflows have only one seed per batch, because they use ComfyUI instead of Diffusers.'

flux.description: Main Flux Dev workflow with smoothing applied. Better for illustrations than flux-nofix.

slot_params.slot8: Upscale factor (float). Bug: /slot8 must NOT be the last parameter before the prompt text — place it earlier in the parameter list to avoid 'Failed to convert an input value to a FLOAT value: factor' error.

known_issues: slot8 position bug: placing /slot8:1.25 as last param before prompt causes 'Failed to convert FLOAT' error. Workaround: move /slot8 earlier in param order.
Hugging Face file renames can break the workflow transiently (Captain: 'Huggingface renamed Flux files, restoring asap').
Does not accept <sdxl> concept tags or /nofix — those are SDXL-specific.
Do not use /strength for txt2img renders.
flux.concept_compatibility: Flux-family LoRAs only (slug suffix -flux). SDXL LoRAs are incompatible.

flux-nofix.description: Same model as flux but without smoothing. Intended for rough/raw photographic output, not illustrations.
known_issues: safetensors file can become corrupted, causing persistent failures. Hampsteri: 'log said safetensors file was corrupted, forced redownload, works now.'
Clip model misconfiguration can cause batch unhandled errors (fixed server-side by Hampsteri Sep 2024).
Not suitable for illustrations — use plain 'flux' instead.
flux-nofix.notes: Hampsteri: 'Flux-nofix isn't great for illustrations, it is intended mainly for rough photograph with less smoothing.'

video.description: Primary video workflow — LTX Video with Flux enhancement. Higher VRAM than video-medium. Intended as placeholder for best available video model.

recommended_params.slot1: Frame count — increase for longer video (e.g. /slot1:200)

known_issues: High fail rate on first attempt — retry often succeeds.
Higher resolutions (above 768x768) frequently fail.
More VRAM-intensive than video-medium.
Prompt engineering critical: use present-tense verbs, camera movement descriptors, restate the subject instead of 'it/her', detailed but not overly long prompts.
prompt_tips: Specify camera movement explicitly (e.g. 'camera pans left', 'slow dolly forward').
Re-state subject name instead of pronouns to avoid subject drift.
Use 'slow motion' or 'very slow movements' to control pace.
Underlying model is LightTricks LTX — long detailed prompts improve output.
Captain: '/guidance:5 and /steps:120 is recommended at low frame counts'

slot_params.slot1: Video length/frame count. Default is short; /slot1:200 for longer video.

video-medium.description: Pure LTX Video without Flux overlay. Lower VRAM, more reliable than 'video' workflow.
video-medium.notes: Preferred when 'video' workflow is failing. Captain: 'the pure model is video-medium'.

video-turbo.description: Faster video generation. Example working prompt: /workflow /run:video-turbo /size:512x512 a duck swimming with a bird

recommended_params.size: 512x512 (safe). For 1024x1024, still use /size:512x512 — providing size helps stop failures.

known_issues: Providing /size helps reduce animation failures and OOM errors.
User tip: 'for an image like 1024x1024, I would still put /size:512x512 because providing size helps a lot to stop the animations from failing or hitting error.'

animate-wan22.description: Wan 2.2 image-to-video. img2vid so you must supply the first frame.
known_issues: Can run closer to 1280x720 max resolution than txt2vid workflows.
Higher resolutions can cause 'smoky glitch' artifact.
/run:animate was previously described as 'pretty much useless' — results can be very on/off depending on server state.
/slot3:false does not reliably turn off the 'ping pong' (reversing loop) effect.

slot_params.slot3: Ping-pong toggle (boolean). Note: false value may not work reliably.

extend-wan22.description: Extend an existing video using Wan 2.2.

ovi-wip.description: Synchronized audio and video generation (WIP). Similar to animate workflows but with audio tags.

special_syntax.escape_requirement: Must use backslash before < and > for the audio tags due to compatibility quirks.

ovi-wip.example_prompt: /wf /run:ovi-wip Close up video of 30yo pirate woman with green hair sitting at park bench. She is reading a newspaper. The woman asks curiously: \<S\>I wonder how much lasagna a cat could eat in a day?\<E\>. She puts down the newspaper and looks into distance. \<AUDCAP\>Curious female voice.\<ENDAUDCAP\>
ovi-wip.notes: Backslash escape before angle brackets is required. This is a WIP workflow.

upscale-supir.description: SUPIR upscaler — high quality but slow.

slot_params.slot8: Upscale multiplier (integer, e.g. /slot8:4 for 4x). Example for print quality: /workflow /run:upscale-remacri /slot8:4

facelift.description: Upscaling with face correction. Three modes via sub-flags.

modes.default: /facelift — upscales, smoothing applied, adjusts eyes and face.
modes.photo: /facelift /photo — upscales, smoothing applied, does NOT alter eyes. Best for preserving high-frequency photo detail.
modes.anime: /facelift /anime — upscales, smoothing applied, does NOT alter eyes. For anime style.

known_issues: /facelift /photo /size:1700x1700 is reliable; 1800x1800 sometimes fails.
Face correction algorithm works poorly on faces rotated 15–45 degrees. Forward-facing or well-lit faces work best.
Captain: 'first try /facelift /size:768x768 to smooth face textures and accentuate eyes' before inpainting.

edit-klein-flux.description: Klein-edited Flux image editing. Supports targeted removal/replacement. Example: /alias /add:removePeople "/wf /run:edit-klein-flux Remove people in the background from the photo"

known_issues: Reported throwing unhandled errors intermittently (Jan 2025). Retry usually works.

dino-inpaint.description: DINO-guided inpainting. Uses /slot1 for the inpaint subject.

slot_params.slot1: Text description of what to inpaint into the detected region.

compose.description: Three-panel composition workflow. Uses /left, /center, /right for each panel prompt.
compose.example_prompt: /compose /size:1984x640 /strength:1.0 /sampler:dpm2m /guidance:2.0 <deliberate2> /nofix /images:1 /steps:10 /center: underwater darkness /left: scary cave entrance /right: yellow submarine with lights

outpaint.notes: Captain: 'slot1 is feathering. slot2/3/4/5 are padding numbered clockwise. Default feather=50. To zoom out only top and bottom: /slot2:200 /slot4:200'

zimage.description: Fast image generation. Primary test workflow per build spec.
zimage.example_prompt: /run:zimage /guidance:7 /seed:42 /images:1 a red circle on white background

prompt_syntax.format: /wf /run:workflow-slug /param1:value1 /param2:value2 positive prompt text [negative prompt in brackets]
prompt_syntax.telegram_prefix: /wf prefix is stripped (Telegram bot command prefix, not an API param)
prompt_syntax.negative_prompt: Text inside [square brackets] at end of prompt. Gets appended to prompt field as '... [negative text]'
prompt_syntax.concept_in_negative: [<concept-name:-2.0>] — negative concept weighting, inside square brackets
prompt_syntax.double_angle_bracket_warning: <<concept>> uses double brackets — these are single concept references, not two. Captain: 'ten cuidado con los doble << porque se usa un par. como: <revani>'

emphasis_syntax.parentheses: ((word)) = stronger emphasis (multiply weight). More parens = more weight.

tips: Recipes (#recipe-name) set defaults. Placing #recipes first, then /guidance:7, lets you override recipe defaults.
Concept tokens and LoRA weight syntax: <concept-name:weight> where weight 0.5-1.2 is typical.
For Flux models: omit /nofix and /clipskip — those are SDXL-specific flags.
For SDXL img2img/remix: /strength:0.5 is the typical safe value. /strength is NOT for txt2img.
For video prompts: use present tense, active verbs, explicit camera direction, restate subject instead of pronouns.
LPW (Long Prompt Weighting) flag: /lpw — enables processing of very long prompts. Does NOT work with LoRAs.
/nofix flag suppresses the automatic quality-fixing post-process step (SDXL-specific).
/parser:new enables the newer prompt parser (for newer SDXL recipes).
/karras enables Karras noise schedule for sampling.
/clipskip:2 skips the last CLIP layer (better for anime/pony models).
/adetailer enables the ADetailer face/hand refinement pass.
Quality boosters for pony models: score_9, score_8_up, score_7_up in positive prompt.
For realistic SDXL: /vae:GraydientPlatformAPI__bright-vae-xl or /vae:madebyollin__sdxl-vae-fp16-fix improves quality.

standard_upscale_pipeline.description: Progressive upscaling from base render to print quality
standard_upscale_pipeline.notes: Eggs Benadryl's proven pipeline. /adetailer can be added at step 2 for face/hand fixing.

print_quality_pipeline.description: Getting images ready for shirt printing or print media

face_inpaint_pipeline.description: Fix face issues in generated images
face_inpaint_pipeline.notes: Captain: 'prepping the image going in can fix issues. Do /facelift /photo /size:768x768 then /inpaint'

img2vid_pipeline.notes: animate-wan22 is img2vid so you must render the first frame manually.

steps: 3. Crop to final monitor dimensions if needed
highres_from_widescreen.notes: Captain: 'start by rendering at /size:2560x720 then facelift to get near monitor size'

triggers: VRAM exhaustion from high /images count or resolution
Wrong model family parameters (e.g. SDXL params on Flux workflow)
remedies: Simply retry — many unhandled errors are transient and self-resolve.
Remove LoRAs one at a time to isolate incompatible combination.
Switch to a different but similar workflow (e.g. video-medium instead of video).
Wait and check /ping — if numbers stay the same, system is stuck and requires admin fix.
unhandled_error.known_stack_trace: utils_clip_embeddings_sdxl.text_embeddings() — indicates SDXL CLIP embedding failure, usually a server-side issue
unhandled_error.retry_philosophy: Captain confirmed renders that appeared to fail often completed successfully. Always check /history before assuming total failure.

triggers: Heavy LoRA stack at high resolution
remedies: Try again after delay (other renders may have freed memory)

corrupted_model.description: Model file on server is corrupted
corrupted_model.remedy: Report to Captain/admin. Hampsteri can force redownload. User cannot fix this.

rendering_now_infinite.description: Render shows 'rendering now' but never completes, no error
triggers: Broken workflow configuration (wrong clip model, missing file)
Wrong model family parameters causing silent failure (e.g. using SDXL params on Flux)
remedies: Wait and retry — provisioning is transient.
Remove incompatible parameters for the workflow's model family.

failed_to_convert_float.description: Parameter parsing failure: 'Failed to convert an input value to a FLOAT value: factor, X.XX'
failed_to_convert_float.trigger: Specific to flux /slot8 when placed as the last parameter before prompt text
failed_to_convert_float.fix: Move /slot8 earlier in the parameter sequence, not immediately before the prompt.

lora_not_applied.description: Render completes but LoRA style not visible in output
triggers: LoRA file silently not loaded (server-side, transient)
LPW (/lpw) flag — LPW does NOT work with LoRAs
lora_not_applied.remedy: Remove /lpw if using LoRAs. Retry if transient.

concept_syntax_error.description: Concept/LoRA not applied due to syntax mistake
concept_syntax_error.common_mistake: Using <<concept>> double brackets instead of <concept> single brackets

video_smoky_glitch.description: Video output has smoky/ghostly visual artifact
video_smoky_glitch.trigger: Resolution too high for video workflow

faceswap_15_broken.description: faceswap / facepush fails with SD 1.5 models
faceswap_15_broken.note: LA Dave confirmed Oct 2024: 'faceswap/facepush broken for 1.5 models. XL works.' Use XL-based models for faceswap workflows.

sdxl.compatible_concepts: SDXL LoRAs and checkpoints. Token suffix -xl indicates SDXL.

flux.compatible_concepts: Flux LoRAs only. Token suffix -flux indicates Flux.

negative_concept_syntax.notes: Negative weights for concepts use double brackets. Weight of -1 to -2 is typical.

stacking_rules: Multiple LoRAs can be stacked but increases failure risk at high /images counts.
Captain: 'an almost endless combination of LoRAs' can cause failures. /images:3 is the safe default.
/lpw (Long Prompt Weighting) is incompatible with LoRAs — choose one or the other.
Concept weights: default is 1.0. Range typically 0.4–1.5. Negative embeddings typically -1.0 to -2.0.
SDXL and Flux LoRAs cannot be mixed in the same render.

sampler_guidance.k_euler: Good default for SD models. Smooth results.
sampler_guidance.k_euler_a: Ancestral variant — more variation between seeds.
sampler_guidance.ddim: Deterministic, consistent. Used for high-step artistic renders.
sampler_guidance.lcm_base: For LCM fast generation. Use with /guidance:1.0 and /steps:4-8.
sampler_guidance.dpm2m_karras: Add /karras flag with /sampler:dpm2m for Karras schedule.

content_type_rules.GET_endpoints: application/vnd.api+json for ALL GET endpoints (/workflows/, /concepts/, /render/{hash}/, /community/). Using application/json returns 406.
content_type_rules.POST_render: application/json for POST /render/. Using vnd.api+json returns 500 with no useful message.

render_body_required_fields.stream: Must be true in body AND Accept: text/event-stream in header. Omitting returns 400.
render_body_required_fields.options_text: Must be present (empty string OK). Python SDK deletes it but TypeScript client must keep it.

api_integration_notes.slug_preservation: Workflow slugs use hyphens. NEVER convert hyphens to underscores. zimage-turbo is correct, zimage_turbo will 404.
api_integration_notes.media_url_source: Rendered media is served from piratediffusion1.s3.amazonaws.com. Must be in CSP img-src and media-src.
api_integration_notes.seed_behavior: ComfyUI workflows: one seed per batch. Diffusers /render: each image gets seed+N.

operational_tips.system_health_check: /ping — if numbers stay constant, system queue is stuck. Requires admin (yanmam or tlack305) to resolve.
operational_tips.history_check: /history — check if render completed even when no bot response received (Telegram API can time out after render completes).
operational_tips.workflow_watcher: cloud.graydient.ai/monitor/workflow/{workflowID}/{runID} — provides ComfyUI node-level error details not shown in bot messages.
operational_tips.render_retry_philosophy: Many transient errors self-resolve on retry. Renders that appear to fail often completed successfully. Always check /history.
operational_tips.model_renaming: Workflows and LoRA slugs can be renamed by admin. Example: copaxtimeless-flux was renamed from a longer slug (Sep 2024). If a known workflow throws 'not found', check current slug list.
operational_tips.video_failure_rate: Video workflows have notably higher failure rates than image workflows (~60% on bad days). Retry is expected behavior.
operational_tips.lora_unavailability: Individual LoRAs can become temporarily corrupted or unavailable. If <specific-lora> fails, substitute a similar one and report to community.
